summary: Completed full frontend testing of the Malasia & Singapur PWA (static client-side app served via http.server on 127.0.0.1:8765). All 13 requested scenarios PASSED with zero console errors and zero page errors. The app loads correctly, renders 33 day-cards (días 0..32), opens day detail with correct events (08:25 llegada, 16:45 MH1336, 19:00 transfer, 21:00 check-in Villa Wanie Inn) and 4 PDF links, supports back navigation, renders Leaflet map with 21 markers + dashed polyline, search returns 5 hits for 'Penang' and clicking one opens that day's detail, converter shows '19.80 EUR' for 100 MYR, expense add/total works (50 MYR -> 9.90 €), checklist has exactly 38 items in 4 categories with persistent localStorage state across reload, frases shows 6 groups & 26 phrases, theme toggle persists in localStorage, service worker registers (state=active), PDF links return 200, no horizontal overflow on 375x812, and day-cards are keyboard-focusable & openable with Enter.

issues: On the mobile viewport the hero overlay text ('Quedan 132 días para el viaje' + 'Inicio: 24 septiembre 2026 · Madrid → Abu Dhabi → Kuala Lumpur') has low contrast over the textured/dark hero background and is partially hard to read. Consider strengthening the darkening gradient/overlay or increasing text shadow on small viewports. NON-BLOCKING.

action_items: (Optional polish) Improve hero text contrast on small screens (mobile 375px) — text over the background image is somewhat hard to read in light theme.
(Optional) Consider adding rel="noopener" verification — already present on doc-link anchors, so OK.
No functional fixes required: every test case from the request is green.
critical_code_review_comments: app.js is 504 lines, single IIFE. Still readable but approaching the threshold; consider splitting into modules (theme, tabs, day, map, tools, search, phrases) if it grows further.

Search.renderSearchResults injects q into innerHTML via the 'Sin resultados para "${q}"' branch without escaping — minor XSS surface, though the highlight() regex escapes special chars. Recommend HTML-escaping q before injecting into the empty-state message.

openDay()/closeDay() use display:none/'' on #hero-today and #day-list instead of toggling a class — fine, but mixing inline styles with CSS state can cause subtle bugs when other JS toggles those nodes. A single 'detail-open' class on a parent would be cleaner.

Currency rates are hardcoded (RATES const). For a 2026 trip this is acceptable per spec (mocked) — but consider adding a visible 'última actualización' label next to '1 € = 5.05 MYR · 1 € = 1.45 SGD'.
Service worker registration silently swallows errors with .catch(() => {}). Logging the error to console would help debugging cache issues.
All interactive elements correctly have data-testid and most have aria-label — accessibility is good.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing /app/malasia/data/itinerary.json (33 días, 21 lugares, 26 documentos).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static PWA at /app/malasia served by python3 -m http.server 8765 (background). Test URL: http://127.0.0.1:8765/index.html. NO REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — pure client-side. Key entry points: index.html, assets/js/app.js (openDay ~L139, closeDay ~L188, initMap ~L197, search ~L362). itinerary.json has días n=0..32 (33 total), 21 lugares with lat/lng, 26 documentos. The fecha-actual heuristic (todayDay()) only matches if local date == an ISO 'fecha' in the list; otherwise the cuenta-atrás hero is shown. The current test run showed 132 días remaining, which means runtime date used by the headless browser is mid-May 2026. Service worker registers correctly; if testing offline mode, navigate twice to ensure cache is populated. localStorage keys used: 'theme', 'checklist', 'expenses'.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 13 acceptance criteria verified end-to-end via Playwright on Chromium headless. The implementation matches the spec precisely (counts of 33 days, 21 markers, 26 phrases in 6 groups, 38 checklist items in 4 categories, conversion 100 MYR → 19.80 EUR, expense 50 MYR → 9.90 €).
